title: nepeta glechoma subsp. hirsuta
intro: Nepeta glechoma subsp. hirsuta, also known as catmint or Serbian catmint, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to Eastern Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Nepeta glechoma subsp. hirsuta include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
